possible_causes.solution: Calibrate accelerometer in Betaflight Setup tab — place drone on flat surface and click Calibrate

possible_causes.cause: Radio not bound or no signal
possible_causes.solution: Bind receiver to transmitter. Verify channels move in Betaflight Receiver tab
Go to Betaflight Modes tab, add ARM on a switch channel (AUX1-4)
Push throttle stick all the way down before arming
If recently crashed, unplug and replug battery — this flag resets

diagnostic_steps: Open Betaflight CLI and type 'status' — shows exact arm-block reasons
Check Receiver tab — move sticks and verify inputs
Check accelerometer calibration in Setup tab

problem: Drone flips over and motors scream on takeoff
symptoms: Motors spin to maximum RPM instantly

possible_causes.solution: Check motor spin in Betaflight Motors tab. FL=CCW, FR=CW, RL=CW, RR=CCW for props-out. 90% of 'turtling' or instant flips are due to the FC expecting a motor to push up while it's pushing down.
Verify if you are running 'Props In' or 'Props Out' in Betaflight. Ensure props match that setting.
Verify the motor numbering (1, 2, 3, 4) matches the Betaflight diagram exactly.
Ensure the FC orientation arrow is pointing forward. Adjust yaw offset in Configuration tab if mounted sideways.

diagnostic_steps: Remove props, spin each motor in Betaflight Motors tab — verify direction
Check prop markings match motor direction
Verify FC arrow points forward

possible_causes.solution: Check ESC signal wire to FC, verify ESC protocol (DSHOT) in Betaflight
Charge battery. Check voltage with multimeter — should be 4.0V+ per cell
Calibrate: max throttle → connect battery → wait for beeps → lower throttle

diagnostic_steps: Count the beep pattern — each brand has specific codes
Check battery voltage with multimeter
Verify signal wire connections between ESC and FC

problem: FPV video has static or interference

possible_causes.cause: VTX and goggles on different channels
possible_causes.solution: Match VTX and goggle channel/band exactly (e.g., Raceband CH3 = 5769MHz)
Inspect antenna connector (SMA/MMCX), replace if damaged
Add low-ESR capacitor (1000μF 35V) across battery pads. Route VTX wires away from motors

diagnostic_steps: Verify goggles on same band/channel as VTX
Inspect antenna for damage
Test at close range — clean = range/power issue

possible_causes.solution: Place drone on perfectly flat surface, calibrate in Betaflight Setup tab
Check physical FC mounting. Use board alignment trims in Betaflight Configuration
Inspect all props and motors. A bent prop or failing bearing causes uneven thrust

diagnostic_steps: Calibrate accelerometer on flat surface
Check Betaflight Setup tab — horizon indicator should be level when drone is flat
Swap props between arms to isolate bad prop

problem: Motors spin but drone has no power

possible_causes.solution: Verify ESC protocol in Betaflight matches ESC firmware (DSHOT300/600)
Check prop direction markings. Curved/beveled edge should lead rotation direction
Check battery voltage — below 3.5V/cell under load means pack is dead
Check ESC amp rating vs motor draw. ESC may be thermally limiting

diagnostic_steps: Check battery voltage under load
Verify prop orientation
In Betaflight Motors tab, test motor spin up to 20% — should spin strong

possible_causes.solution: Try a different USB cable — must be a DATA cable, not charge-only
Install Zadig drivers (WinUSB) or STM32 VCP drivers for your FC
If LED shows DFU mode, flash firmware via DFU in Configurator Firmware Flasher
Inspect solder joints on USB connector. Re-solder or use UART-to-USB adapter as backup

diagnostic_steps: Try different USB cable (data cable)
Check Device Manager for 'STM32' or 'DFU' device
Try different USB port on computer

possible_causes.cause: RX antenna damaged or poorly mounted
possible_causes.solution: Check antenna solder joint. Mount antenna vertical, away from carbon fiber. Use antenna tube

possible_causes.cause: Wrong RF power setting on TX
possible_causes.solution: Check TX module power output. ELRS: ensure 250mW+. FrSky: check power setting in module
If using diversity RX, ensure both antennas are connected and at 90° to each other
Separate RX antenna from VTX antenna by at least 3cm. Use different polarization

diagnostic_steps: Check RSSI/LQ in OSD at increasing distances
Inspect antenna physical condition
Test with VTX off to rule out interference

symptoms: Horizontal lines as soon as motors arm

possible_causes.solution: Solder a Low ESR Capacitor (e.g., 35V 1000uF) directly to the main battery pads to filter spikes.
Ensure your signal wires (Video and Ground) are twisted together to shield them from motor noise.
Ensure VTX and Camera share the same ground point on the FC.

diagnostic_steps: Check if noise increases with throttle
Verify capacitor is present and soldered well
Twist video signal and ground wires together

possible_causes.solution: Practice softer landings. Cut throttle 1-2 feet above ground and let it drop
Use quality brands: HQ Prop, Gemfan, DAL. Avoid no-name props from random sellers
Tighten prop nuts firmly but not over-torque. Use nylock nuts — they self-lock

diagnostic_steps: Inspect prop material — polycarbonate is more flexible than nylon
Check prop nut tightness
Consider prop guards for indoor flying

symptoms: Motor feels rough when spun by hand

possible_causes.solution: Replace motor bearings or replace the entire motor. Bearings degrade from crash landings
Remove motor bell (C-clip), clean with compressed air, remove grass/dirt
Spin motor slowly — if bell wobbles visually, shaft is bent. Replace motor or shaft

diagnostic_steps: Spin motor by hand — should be smooth with no gritty feeling
Remove bell and inspect for debris
Check shaft straightness by visual inspection during slow spin

possible_causes.cause: Battery end of life (high internal resistance)
possible_causes.solution: Check cell IR on charger. If >10mΩ per cell or cells mismatched, retire the pack

possible_causes.cause: Motor or PID issue causing excessive current
possible_causes.solution: Check motor temperatures post-flight. Hot motors = excessive current. Lower D gains or fix vibration
Check motor idle (min throttle) in Betaflight. Lower digital idle if motors spin fast at rest

diagnostic_steps: Compare flight time across different batteries
Check cell IR on charger after cooling
Monitor current draw in Betaflight OSD

problem: OSD not displaying on FPV feed

possible_causes.solution: Go to Betaflight OSD tab and enable elements. Ensure at least battery voltage is checked

possible_causes.cause: OSD chip on FC not connected to camera path
possible_causes.solution: Verify camera video-in goes to FC OSD input, and FC OSD output goes to VTX video-in
Upload correct font via Betaflight OSD tab → Font Manager. Ensure font matches display type

diagnostic_steps: Check OSD tab in Betaflight — enable some elements
Verify physical wiring: camera → FC → VTX
Try uploading font via Font Manager

problem: GPS won't get a satellite fix

possible_causes.cause: GPS module obstructed by carbon fiber
possible_causes.solution: Mount GPS on a raise with clear sky view — above the frame using a mast/pedestal
Check Betaflight Ports tab — GPS must be on correct UART at 115200 baud

possible_causes.cause: GPS first cold start takes time
possible_causes.solution: Allow 2-5 minutes for cold start fix. Subsequent fixes (warm start) are faster
Mount GPS far from ESCs and VTX. Use shielding or ferrite bead on GPS cable

diagnostic_steps: Check GPS tab in Betaflight — verify module is communicating
Wait 5 minutes outdoors with clear sky
Check wiring: TX→RX, RX→TX between GPS and FC
